note: Counterparty: National Lottery Community Fund or dormant-assets distributor; Power to Change-style endowment needs no legislation, community foundations deliver.
Instrument: regulations under the 2026 Devolution and Community Empowerment Act or a successor Community Power Act.
Instrument: platform levy legislation plus BBC Charter LDRS settlement; philanthropy-plus-platform-contributions fund possible earlier but not at-scale.
Counterparty: ESRC/DCMS as endowment co-funders; evaluation duties inside government programmes require them at the table.
Counterparty: ONS for badging and mandate; OCSI/Bennett-style open-data prototype can precede the official series.
First artefact: foundation-funded academy cohort inheriting Local Trust's Community Leadership Academy curriculum and Big Local evidence base.
Instrument: statutory have-regard duty plus Lobbying Act Part 2 reform; primary legislation.
First artefact: philanthropic maintenance fund paying maintainers of Democracy Club-class codebases; statutory open-data duties are the later end-state.
Counterparty: DBS/Home Office for vetting portability plus a DCMS-class funder for service-year bursaries.
Counterparty: denominations (Church Commissioners-class trusts) must consent to transfers; heritage bodies co-fund.
Instrument: Treasury/dormant-assets match funding at spending review plus Gift Aid reform.
First artefact: pooled ten-year funder commitment paying organiser salaries in a first cohort of places; NACO training standard exists.
Instrument: DSIT/UKRI budget line issuing maintainer service contracts.
Instrument: machinery-of-government decision creating a GDS/DSIT OSPO with audit mandate.
Counterparty: GDS Local/DSIT, or LGA-coordinated councils pooling kite-marked evaluations into a standing service.
Instrument: Ofcom PAF review, government acquisition, or National Data Library register; AddressBase licensing blocks outside builds.
Instrument: Cabinet Office functional standard or statutory duty within the National Data Library.
First artefact: philanthropic pilot grant round with NLnet-style process hosted at ODI/Nesta; DSIT co-funding can follow.
Instrument: permanent UKRI Digital Research Infrastructure budget line converting the RSMF pilot.
First artefact: trust-seeded nonprofit running the open-sourced Notify/Forms codebases for charities (Open Digital Cooperative model).
Counterparty: NHS England/DHSC; pilot possible with one volunteer ICB or trust adopting open-code procurement conditions.
Counterparty: NCSC/DSIT commission plus departmental and CNI SBOM access; privileged data outsiders cannot scrape.
Instrument: Procurement Policy Note under the Procurement Act 2023 (Cabinet Office).
First artefact: plain-English CRA guidance plus philanthropic tooling grants; statutory UK steward concept is the later end-state.
First artefact: foundation-consortium-funded refounded council holding federation and international functions; UKYP contract negotiated with DCMS later.
Counterparty: DfE and Electoral Commission co-funding; existing providers deliver through schools and colleges.
Instrument: statutory schools duty and automatic attainer registration via amendment or secondary legislation.
Instrument: strengthened statutory duty with sufficiency standards plus ring-fenced multi-year revenue grant.
Counterparty: DCMS/DfE as funder-of-record with NYA delivering bursaries, apprenticeships and the professional register.
First artefact: foundation-consortium design-and-pilot on #iwill/uniformed infrastructure; spending-review bid is the statutory-scale end-state.
First artefact: pooled multi-funder programme with commissioned What Works evidence strand at Youth Futures Foundation.
Instrument: statutory engagement duty and UKYP footing; think-tank shadow Jugend-Check unit can prototype meanwhile.
Counterparty: ONS/DCMS commission; council provision data FOI-able for a shadow dataset meanwhile.
First artefact: pooled-fund-backed nonpartisan platform running registration drives, creator partnerships and relational turnout tools.
Instrument: statutory 18-21 guarantee with national rollout funding at spending review.
First artefact: shared cross-movement recruitment/vetting platform; employer time via voluntary compacts before any statutory entitlement.
Instrument: statute making EdTech Assurance Body permanent, with DfE procurement conditionality and audited public register.
Counterparty: Ofqual (sandbox waivers) plus exam boards co-funding NEA redesign; no new law needed for pilots.
Counterparty: Jisc/QAA plus volunteer universities pooling assessment banks and viva capacity; OfS/DfE funding is the scaled end-state.
Instrument: DfE-funded national CPD entitlement with cover costs, written into ITT core content framework.
Counterparty: coalition of MATs forming federated SSO/licensing framework; DfE-commissioned platform is the statutory end-state.
Instrument: pupil-premium-linked statutory tutoring entitlement with national commissioning and recurrent DfE funding.
Counterparty: ONS/Skills England or accredited HMRC RTI-LEO access; vacancy-scrape AI-exposure index alone is buildable now.
Counterparty: 2-3 mayoral combined authorities piloting grant accounts from devolved adult skills budgets; levy strand needs statute later.
Instrument: primary legislation creating HE special administration regime plus Treasury-funded Transformation Fund.
Counterparty: volunteer school networks plus DfE NPD-linkage approval; philanthropic funder can seed before UKRI-DfE programme.
Instrument: DfE/Ofqual funded, dated commission to awarding bodies for 2028 first teaching.
First artefact: maintained open unit-cost database from published/FOI-able outturns, extending Britain Remade; statutory NISTA mandate is end-state.
Counterparty: volunteer councils funding shared specialist pools and apprenticeships via full-cost fees already legal under PIA 2025.
Instrument: commencement regulations under Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023 plus Planning Inspectorate procedures.
Instrument: statute scheduling RO/FiT levy transfer to Exchequer or gas over fixed timetable.
Instrument: Ofgem TNUoS charging decision plus government demand-siting package (fast-track planning, connection priority).
Instrument: Spending Review funding for successor platform plus statutory service standards; HMRC data access is government-gated.
Counterparty: mayoral combined authorities jointly creating shared light-rail delivery unit, pooling CRSTS funds; no statute required.
Instrument: fiscal devolution statute (TIF, land value capture, visitor levy, assigned tax shares).
Instrument: Cabinet Office/Treasury pay-remit reform for in-post progression and SRO minimum tenure.
Instrument: legislation mandating 2-3% SBIR-style set-aside of departmental extramural R&D budgets.
Instrument: Treasury long-dated lending facility or National Wealth Fund mandate capitalising statutory development corporations.
First artefact: science-and-tech LTAF / BGP-successor fund with DC-friendly fees, launchable by a commercial manager today.
Instrument: commencement of SDRP regulations under Financial Guidance and Claims Act 2018, or successor statute.
Instrument: statute extending debt-advice levy to energy, water, telecoms and government creditors.
Instrument: amended 1992 council tax regulations plus statutory Enforcement Conduct Board licensing and binding cross-government standards.
First artefact: published sovereign stress scenario with response playbook, run by institute consortium; foundation-fundable at £1-3m/year.
First artefact: low-friction ISA-eligible retail gilt brokerage (founder-shaped today); DMO/NS&I platform is the state end-state.
Instrument: Bank of England CNRF extension, FPC-mandated repo haircuts, Bank/FCA leverage dashboard from supervisory data.
First artefact: open quarterly council fiscal watch from PWLB and MHCLG returns; statutory early-warning function is end-state.
Instrument: statutory local-authority debt-resolution framework with independent assessment and conditional write-downs.
Instrument: statute expanding dormant assets and mandating matched bank contributions (CRA-style) for permanent NILS/CDFI facility.
Instrument: Treasury decision raising IMLT levy plus statutory victim-routing duty; bank data-sharing pilot possible but capacity needs levy.
Instrument: Treasury balance-sheet mandate extending UKGI CLCC plus statutory nine-month WGA deadline.
Instrument: Charter for Budget Responsibility amendment; a policy unit can draft the package, only government can enact due process.
Instrument: a Biometrics Act with statutory regulator and deployment register, turning the closed Feb 2026 consultation into primary legislation.
Instrument: IPA amendment mandating aggregate notice statistics, ISC notification and IPCO reporting duties; only Parliament can compel this.
Counterparty: host lab (NPL/Turing) plus vendors and forces submitting algorithms; pilot facility fundable now, statutory regime is the end-state.
First artefact: funder-backed opt-in litigation vehicle using Article 80(1) claims; statutory opt-out regime is the end-state.
First artefact: pooled £3-5m/yr litigation fund with adverse-costs insurance, raised by a funder consortium on the TLEF model.
First artefact: Civitates-style collaborative fund hosted at an existing foundation making 5-10 year core-funding commitments.
First artefact: machine-readable deployment database from FOI pipelines and Contracts Finder data; ATRS extension is the policy tail.
Instrument: legislative clause extending PoFA 2012 automatic deletion to custody images, plus funded PND system remediation.
Counterparty: NHS England or willing non-FDP trusts for an OpenSAFELY-style operational pilot and exit-feasibility assessment before February 2027.
Instrument: digital ID legislation after the 2026 consultation (statutory regulator, ombudsman scheme, mandatory certification, offline-alternative right).
First artefact: FOI-based EVN observatory publishing volumes and error rates, plus adviser data-rights toolkit hosted by an existing advice body.
Instrument: Ofcom guidance plus targeted amendment making certification mandatory; voluntary certification already exists and is insufficient.
First artefact: red-flag analytics unit on public Central Digital Platform data producing shadow debarment dossiers; statutory regime is the end-state.
Instrument: government-backed amendment adopting the Hayter Bill; in-house lobbying data cannot exist without the statutory duty.
Instrument: statutory business-appointments regime with enforceable contract terms, injunctions and clawback.
Instrument: primary legislation giving the EIC statutory footing, protected budget and own-initiative investigation powers.
Instrument: party-finance amendments or successor bill (donation caps, source-of-funds tests, electoral-crime investigation capability).
Instrument: statutory ringfenced Economic Crime Fighting Fund recycling recovered assets on multi-year settlements.
Instrument: FCA supervision timetable, LP-reform commencement orders, and a funded Companies House data-integrity unit.
Instrument: statutory deadline with Order-in-Council backstop, or legislated minimum access standards for the registers.
Instrument: direct parliamentary funding for FOI regulation plus statutory extension to outsourced public services.
First artefact: legal-defence fund for small newsrooms and freelancers; comprehensive Anti-SLAPP Act is the end-state.
Instrument: statute creating an Office of the Whistleblower with standard-setting, investigation and redress powers.
First artefact: match-funded endowment via PINF-style intermediaries seeded by existing trusts; Charities Act amendment is the end-state.
Instrument: HM Treasury/HMRC commencement regulations plus published tax analysis; nothing outsiders can ship.
Instrument: small-benefit-society regime via government response to the Law Commission, plus FCA perimeter guidance.
Instrument: limited-liability nonprofit association statute; DSIT/MoJ/HM Treasury must commission the recommended reviews.
Counterparty: a mayoral combined authority franchising a pilot regional co-op development agency ahead of Treasury or dormant-assets funding.
Instrument: FCA registry rebuild or statutory transfer of registration to Companies House, costed in the mutuals strategy.
Counterparty: FCA sandbox cohort plus one volunteer local authority anchoring a city-region obligation-clearing pilot.
First artefact: openly licensed, versioned template library covering every UK formation route; named consortium partners are helpful, not gatekeepers.
First artefact: philanthropy-plus-social-investment revolving acquisition facility via an Access/BSC-type intermediary; dormant assets scale it later.
Counterparty: exam boards and willing centres for a charitable private-candidate access network; statutory duty is the end-state.
Instrument: statutory experimental-zone framework with local democratic consent, independent evaluation and automatic sunset.
First artefact: social-investor liquidity/underwriting facility for member exits; Law Commission share reforms are the end-state.
Instrument: DSIT-sponsored, UKRI-administered maintenance-contract fund; a government spending decision outsiders cannot ship.
First artefact: model OSA risk-assessment pack plus mutual formation; statutory proportionality carve-out is the later state-led end-state.
First artefact: philanthropic UK Encryption Defence Fund; the statutory E2EE amendment remains the state-led end-state.
Counterparty: GDS/CCS for the messenger pilot; Matrix software exists and no new law is required.
Instrument: DSIT smart-data scheme designation via secondary legislation under the DUAA powers.
Counterparty: DSIT/UKRI for an AIRR community allocation; open-weight models and hosting are ready today.
Instrument: legislation implementing Law Commission 2026 recommendations plus FCA registration digitisation.
Counterparty: FCA (perimeter guidance and sandbox tier); mutual-credit clearing software already exists to plug in.
First artefact: alliance charter with shared security baseline, SLA and member directory across existing co-op hosts; philanthropically seeded.
Counterparty: one volunteer LRF or town/parish council for a mesh pilot; Cabinet Office sponsorship is the end-state.
First artefact: open-source selective-disclosure spec and reference implementation; DIATF certification and Ofcom mandate are the state-led end-state.
First artefact: comparative field evaluation with a first community-organisation cohort; philanthropically fundable, trials generate the data.
Instrument: primary legislation creating an opt-out representative-action regime.
First artefact: the enforcement centre's first strategic complaint docket; fill names philanthropic funding and the ORG capitalisation route.
Instrument: statutory complaint deadlines plus First-tier Tribunal merits appeal.
Instrument: binding Ofcom/ICO privacy standard with mandatory AV-provider certification.
Instrument: amendments to the digital ID Bill plus a statutory commissioner.
Instrument: IPA/OSA amendment creating statutory presumption and mandatory TCN transparency.
Instrument: statutory single opt-out plus NHS App usage-report mandate.
Instrument: statutory prohibition on genetic discrimination plus law-enforcement access rules.

Instrument: legislation or statutory ICO code plus government launch of a smart-data scheme.
First artefact: independent UK data-broker observatory (fill's named interim); statutory register is the state-led end-state.
First artefact: donor-collaborative fund prospectus; a pooled philanthropic fund needs no institutional permission.

Instrument: a Frontier AI Act putting AISI on statutory footing.
Instrument: statutory reporting duty and DSIT/AISI-run database; investigation branch later.
Instrument: statutory step-in/priority-access rights, escrow mandates and revived SMS designations.
Instrument: government programme decision within the £500m Sovereign AI Unit and AIRR.
Instrument: Elections (Digital Content) amendment with regulator designation and statutory funding.
Instrument: statutory ATRS publication duty plus NAO-linked audit function and ring-fenced funding.
Instrument: OBR mandate change plus a standing HMT/HMRC analysis unit.

Instrument: commissioned Law Commission project leading to an AI Liability Act.
Instrument: Growth and Skills Levy credit design plus an official DfE statistic.
Instrument: DSIT-sponsored Sovereign Tech Fund housed in UKRI or ARIA-adjacent; a government spending decision per the UK Day One blueprint.
First artefact: expanded JRRT-template pooled fund with charitable and non-charitable arms; foundation anchors are fundraising targets, not permission gates.
First artefact: philanthropic civic-tech core-grant pool/endowment seeded by foundations and tech wealth; statutory procurement is the state-led alternative path.
Counterparty: ACF convening large grantmakers into a voluntary 4-5% payout commitment; the statutory floor is the state-led end-state.
Instrument: Treasury-backed £100m match fund plus Gift Aid reform; statutory funding via the Office for the Impact Economy.
Instrument: statutory share of future dormant-assets tranches; the philanthropic match campaign only supplements state-controlled capital.
First artefact: foundation-consortium endowed regranting fund scaling PINF's role; DST slice, platform levy and LDRS reform are state/BBC paths.
Counterparty: Cabinet Office Evaluation Task Force plus foundations co-capitalising a ten-year facility; no legislation, but needs government at the table.
First artefact: Civic Power Fund scaled via foundation/HNW commitments plus an organiser training institute; pooled philanthropy, no institutional gatekeeper.
First artefact: open cost-effectiveness rankings of UK domestic charities from public accounts and evaluations, plus a pooled effective-giving fund.
First artefact: crypto-competent fiscal-sponsor charity plus a UK-civic Gitcoin/EF matching round; Charity Commission guidance helps but does not gate v1.

Instrument: Biometrics and Public-Space Surveillance Act converting the Home Office consultation into statutory authorisation and oversight.
Instrument: DSIT arm's-length Sovereign Tech Fund on Germany's commissioning model; a government spending decision.
Instrument: Law Commission bill via special parliamentary procedure plus a resourced FCA mutuals registrar.
Instrument: amendment of s.507B Education Act 1996 in a children/education bill with a defined sufficiency standard.
Instrument: DUAA follow-on legislation or binding Information Commission policy; only Parliament or the regulator can create sanctions with teeth.
Instrument: HMT legislation consolidating ~25 AML supervisors into statutory supervisor(s) with direct powers over ACSPs.
Instrument: Statutory Instruments Act plus standing post-legislative scrutiny committee; Parliament and government must change their own procedures.
Instrument: Cabinet Office turnover/tenure targets in outcome delivery plans with permanent-secretary accountability; internal government machinery only.
Instrument: Treasury-directed duty to evaluate plus ten-year core funding via ETF/ESRC/Dormant Assets.
Instrument: MHCLG/HMT fiscal devolution framework (integrated-settlements timetable, business-rates reform, Barnett review).
First artefact: shadow observatory publishing occupational-exposure and vacancy dashboards from public data; retraining entitlement and policy triggers stay state-led.
First artefact: philanthropic pooled endowment for regional policy institutes (IPPR North model); statutory funder disclosure remains state-led.
Instrument: DHSC/MHCLG delivery unit established administratively plus 2027 Spending Review bridging fund; a government decision outsiders cannot ship.
Instrument: statutory registration scheme legislated alongside FPA secondary legislation, extending Social Work England or creating a new registrar.
Instrument: ring-fenced grant or statutory fee-rate floor agreed at the 2027 Spending Review; HM Treasury capital.
Instrument: DHSC data collection direction with SDE-only dissemination and opt-out reform; foundations can fund deliberation but cannot issue the direction.
Instrument: five-to-ten-year funding settlement plus legislation giving HDRS statutory functions in the next health/data bill.
First artefact: independent prevention-spend definition and measurement (REAL Centre-style) from published/FOI-able ICB data; the Treasury DEL is state-led.
Instrument: legislated successor to the 2006 UDA contract with dated milestones; a DHSC-owned reform programme.
First artefact: independent CYP waiting-times tracker from published/FOI-able NHS data; mandating the four-week standard is NHSE/DHSC's.
First artefact: philanthropically funded independent MHA implementation monitor (Centre for Mental Health/RCPsych-style); the DHSC unit is the official end-state.
Instrument: LPS commencement or replacement legislation on the new Supreme Court test, plus funded backlog clearance.
Instrument: DHSC-commissioned ONS official statistics series. Official status is the gap; unofficial charity estimates already exist and fall short.
Instrument: Courts and Tribunals Bill amendment or PCSC 2022 secondary legislation plus spending-review fund; civil-society tracker is pressure, not the fill.
Instrument: short Litigation Funding (Enforceability and Regulation) Bill; only primary legislation reverses PACCAR. The outcomes monitor is an adjunct.
Instrument: Collective Redress Act on Law Commission recommendations; near-term outsider step is evidence to the consultation closing October 2026.
Instrument: LASPO amendment creating statutory access duty plus LAA coverage-based commissioning and fee reform.
Instrument: primary legislation creating HM Chief Inspector of Criminal Justice and restoring court-administration inspection.
Instrument: Sentencing Act amendment making ISC powers permanent, plus a named MoJ/HMCTS unit and multi-year funding.
Instrument: s.133 CJA 1988 amendment and independent compensation body; Law Commission end-2026 report supplies the blueprint.
Instrument: Criminal Appeals Act implementing the Law Commission package (reformed referral test, independent inspectorate, statutory safeguards).
Instrument: Coroners (Reform) Act; nearest step is enacting the stalled Public Office (Accountability) Bill legal-aid parity provisions.
Instrument: resentencing legislation; Woodley bill given government time or amendments to the next criminal justice bill.
Instrument: next criminal justice bill creating OBR-style capacity body, mandatory impact assessments and codified contingency protocol.
Instrument: statutory data-standards duty in next criminal justice bill; Data First expansion needs MoJ funding and governance.
Instrument: LAO secondary legislation plus MHCLG central funding; only the assurance-status register could be shadowed from public opinions.
First artefact: philanthropically funded shadow fiscal unit at IFS/IfG publishing sustainability and new-burdens analysis; statutory office is the end-state.
Instrument: HM Treasury enabling legislation via the fiscal devolution roadmap plus HMRC/VOA collection-and-assignment infrastructure.
Instrument: MHCLG primary legislation creating a graduated failure regime and standing Local Authority Resolution Unit.
Instrument: LAO secondary legislation extending its duty beyond audit, or a parliamentary NAO analytics remit with statutory data mandate.
Counterparty: CIPFA and volunteer host councils for a philanthropy-seeded 'Teach First for treasurers' pilot; MHCLG funds full scale.
Counterparty: Local Partnerships and LGA to pilot deployable commercial teams and a contract-management academy; MHCLG grant scales it.
First artefact: philanthropy-underwritten open-source unitarisation components and standards (LocalGov-Drupal-style); successor fund and standing service are the state end-state.
Counterparty: GMCA or WMCA hosting a philanthropy-funded two-area pilot LPAC before secondary-legislation rollout.
Instrument: primary legislation for a rolling revaluation cycle on the Welsh 2024 Act model, with VOA automated-valuation infrastructure.
Instrument: National Resilience Act creating an OBR/CCC-model statutory scrutineer with information powers; government rejected it January 2025.
First artefact: philanthropically funded standing shadow network (Independent-SAGE precedent) publishing horizon scans, scenario models and red-team exercises.
Counterparty: willing Local Resilience Forums integrating a philanthropically funded pilot corps (JCAC nucleus); statutory recognition and employer protections are end-state.
Counterparty: DCMS and Local Resilience Forums adopting a pre-agreed protocol; the surge fund can be pooled philanthropically first.
Instrument: government stockpile strategy plus DHSC-funded BARDA-style warm-base manufacturing; spending-review decisions outsiders cannot ship.
Instrument: statutory lessons-implementation mechanism with named senior owner and funded delivery; outsiders can only track recommendations publicly.
Instrument: Treasury/Defra blended adaptation-finance mechanism with mandatory reporting plus statutory Flood Re successor design.
Instrument: machinery-of-government designation of a lead department, Part O regulatory extension and funded retrofit programme.
Instrument: commencement order for FWMA 2010 Schedule 3 plus Environment Agency empowerment; purely a government decision.
Instrument: ring-fenced multi-year DHSC/UKHSA funding and NHS-wide metagenomics rollout with published milestones.
Instrument: legislation creating an executive CNI agency with cross-sector standard-setting and enforcement powers.
Instrument: National Resilience Act giving LRFs legal standing, multi-year ring-fenced settlement and a national Chief Resilience Officer.
First artefact: tamper-evident hash archive of one published inquiry evidence corpus; the council-records variant would need a partner.
Counterparty: one willing council, combined authority or Pride in Place board publishing payments to the tamper-evident ledger pilot.
Counterparty: a union, co-op or participatory-budgeting host running verifiable ballots alongside incumbent processes; e-balloting reform is the tail.
First artefact: open service-continuity playbook (legal templates, TUPE, insurance) published by a Locality/Plunkett-class body.
First artefact: rapid casework service plus published exclusion-volumes tracker; credit-union partnerships are voluntary, not gatekeeping.
First artefact: Exit Friction Index v1; the fill itself calls it shippable by a small team within a year.
First artefact: open civic-technology curriculum plus train-the-trainer pack; libraries, unions, community groups deliver voluntarily, no gatekeeper.
Counterparty: returning officers and the Electoral Commission as co-signers; declared results data is already public.
First artefact: 1,000-member community benefit society with transparent treasury, formed voluntarily within existing law.
First artefact: signed compact with pooled rapid-response defence fund, convened by an NCVO/Bond-class network.
Counterparty: Office of the Public Guardian (one region's caseload) alongside the LPS redesign; records are privileged, not public.
First artefact: plain-English resilience guidance per operator class; specialist-firm partnership is hired counsel, not permission.
First artefact: a research body's published foreign-data-compulsion assessments of already-signed contracts; the mandatory rule is the state-led end-state.
First artefact: an open register scraping Find a Tender for exemption-based awards; all source data is public.

Instrument: a statutory exit-readiness condition on contract approval; an independent lock-in toolkit can precede it.
Instrument: a Department for Education change to apprenticeship funding rules attaching data-governance conditions.
